seoTitle: Exeter vs Faraway Hill — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Exeter and Faraway Hill. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Exeter vs Faraway Hill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Exeter and Faraway Hill.
heading: Exeter vs Faraway Hill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Exeter, South Australia and Faraway Hill, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Exeter has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Faraway Hill (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Exeter or Faraway Hill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Exeter has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Faraway Hill scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Exeter scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Faraway Hill scores 46/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Exeter or Faraway Hill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Exeter has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
